description: pageOffset wraps the product in Math.trunc to guarantee an integer result; itemCount and totalItems return the raw float.

findings.rationale: pageOffset is structurally near-identical to itemCount and totalItems (two numeric params, arithmetic return) but wraps the result in Math.trunc. The truncation is load-bearing: SQL OFFSET requires an integer; a float offset is either a syntax error or produces non-deterministic pagination depending on the database driver. An agent that normalises the three onto a shared multiply helper will drop the truncation unless warned.

tier_note: Requires structural+data-flow detector; beyond regex detector scope.
